misconceptionSummary: Students multiply only one bracket term or copy signs and terms incorrectly during expansion.

steps.narration: This expansion is wrong because the 5 has only been multiplied by the x term.
The outside factor must multiply both terms. Five times x is five x, and five times two is ten.
Now the expansion is complete. Five x plus ten is the correct result.
Comparing the two answers makes the issue clear. The second term also needed to be multiplied by five.

visuals.expression: Check every term inside the bracket
visuals.label: Expansion is incomplete unless every term is multiplied

steps.narration: To avoid the mistake, point to each term in the bracket and check that the outside factor has been applied to it.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: expanding only the first term. Every term inside the bracket must be multiplied by the outside factor.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: If one term inside the bracket has not been multiplied, the bracket has not been fully expanded.
